Mo To Expunge Lis Pendes And For Atty Fees
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NISSIM LANYADOO Motion to Expunge Lis Pendes and for Attorney Fees. Appearances: Ann McFarland Draper, Esq. of Draper Weaver LLP (415) 391-4200 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; A. Jeanne Jorge, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellamn, Melbostad, Gibson & Harris, LLP (415) 673-5600, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to expunge lis pendes is granted, request for attorney fees is denied. Complaint contains a real property claim for reconveyance. Plaintiffs fails to carry their burden to establish the probable validity of the reconveyance claim. An accounting at the time of foreclosure indicates a principal balance of $778,959 on the loan. No evidence supports any deduction applied to the interest on the note. Plaintiff admits Campbell is a licensed real estate broker, and evidence indicates Campbell arranged the loan and was paid to do so. Plaintiff admits receiving notice of trustee sale which warns Plaintiff is in default and unless he takes action, property may be sold. In light of plaintiff's beliefs about the note being satisfied, plaintiffs acted in substantial justification in recording lis pendens. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
